extensionShortHeaders.name: AdvancedHTTP
extensionShortHeaders.shortDescription: HTTP requests with custom headers, methods, caching, CORS bypass, and JSON/FormData body.

eventsFunctions.name: CreateRequest
eventsFunctions.description: Creates a template for your request. All requests must be made from a request template.

eventsFunctions.name: CopyRequest
eventsFunctions.description: Creates a new request template with all the attributes from an existing one.

eventsFunctions.name: SetRequestMethod
eventsFunctions.description: The HTTP method of the request. GET is the default and what you should use if you are unsure which to pick. A request to a REST API endpoint may have a different effect depending on the method - refer to the documentation of the API you are calling to learn about the appropriate method to use.

eventsFunctions.name: RequestMethod
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the HTTP method of the request. GET is the default and what you should use if you are unsure which to pick. A request to a REST API endpoint may have a different effect depending on the method - refer to the documentation of the API you are calling to learn about the appropriate method to use.

eventsFunctions.name: SetRequestCache
eventsFunctions.description:
Defines to what extent the results of the request is can/must be cached. When cached, instead of sending a request to the server, the browser will avoid making a real request to the server and will use a previous response given by the server for the same request.

The server also has a say in this via the Cache-Control header.

eventsFunctions.name: RequestCache
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the HTTP method of the request. GET is the default and what you should use if you are unsure which to pick. A request to a REST API endpoint may have a different effect depending on the method - refer to the documentation of the API you are calling to learn about the appropriate method to use.

eventsFunctions.name: SetRequestBody
eventsFunctions.description: Change the body of the HTTP request. Contains data to send to the server, ususally in plain text, JSON or FormData format. This cannot be set for GET requests.

eventsFunctions.name: SetJSONRequestBody
eventsFunctions.description: Sets the body of an HTTP request to a JSON representation of a structure variable.

eventsFunctions.name: SetFormDataRequestBody
eventsFunctions.description: Sets the body of an HTTP request to a form data representation of a structure variable.

eventsFunctions.name: RequestBody
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the body of the HTTP request. Contains data to send to the server, ususally in plain text, JSON or FormData format. This cannot be set for GET requests.

eventsFunctions.name: SetRequestHeader
eventsFunctions.description: Change an HTTP header to be sent with the request.

eventsFunctions.name: RequestHeader
eventsFunctions.description: Compare an HTTP header to be sent with the request.

eventsFunctions.name: SetRequestURL
eventsFunctions.description: Change the request template's target URL.

eventsFunctions.name: RequestURL
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the request template's target URL.

eventsFunctions.name: UseCORSBypass
eventsFunctions.description:
CORS prevents most external websites from being queried with the browser's HTTP client, since the browser may be authenticated on that website and as such another website would be able to impersonate the player on that other website.

When the CORS Bypass is enabled, the request will be made from a server that is not authenticated anywhere and as such is not blocked by CORS, and it will share the response with your game. Note that as such, authentication cookies are ignored! If you own the REST API you are requesting, add CORS headers to your server instead of using this CORS Bypass.

eventsFunctions.name: IsUsingCORSBypass
eventsFunctions.description: Checks whether or not CORS Bypass has been enabled for the request template.

eventsFunctions.name: ExecuteRequest
eventsFunctions.description: Executes the request defined by a request template.

eventsFunctions.name: ResponseSuccess
eventsFunctions.description: Checks whether the server marked the response as a success (status code 1XX/2XX), not as a failure (status code 4XX/5XX).

eventsFunctions.name: ResponseStatusCode
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the status code of the HTTP request (e.g. 200 if succeeded, 404 if not found, etc).

eventsFunctions.name: ResponseStatusText
eventsFunctions.description: Gets the status text for a response. For example, for a response with the status code 404, the status text will be "Not Found".

eventsFunctions.name: ResponseHeader
eventsFunctions.description: Compare one of the HTTP headers included in the server's response.

eventsFunctions.name: ReadResponseText
eventsFunctions.description: Reads the body sent by the server, and store it as a string in a variable.

eventsFunctions.name: ReadResponseJSON
eventsFunctions.description: Reads the body sent by the server, parses it as JSON and stores the resulting structure in a variable.

extensionShortHeaders.name: Bounce
extensionShortHeaders.shortDescription: Bounce objects off collisions using forces. Not for Physics engine or Platformer.

eventsBasedBehaviors.description: Provides an action to make the object bounce from another object it just touched. Add a permanent force to the object and, when in collision with another one, use the action to make it bounce realistically.
eventsBasedBehaviors.name: Bounce

eventsFunctions.name: BounceOff
eventsFunctions.description:
Bounce the object off another object it is currently colliding with, according to the angle and the speed of forces applied on the object.
Make sure to test for a collision between the two objects before launching this action. All the forces will be removed from the object, and a new permanent force will be added to make the object bounce.

eventsFunctions.name: BounceOffSpecificAngle
eventsFunctions.fullName: Bounce off another object toward a specified angle
eventsFunctions.description:
Bounce the object off another object it is currently colliding with, according to the angle and the speed of forces applied on the object.
The bounce will always be calculated *to go toward the specified angle (the "normal angle")*. For example, if the object is arriving at this exact angle, it will bounce in the opposite direction.

Make sure to test for a collision between the two objects before launching this action. All the forces will be removed from the object, and a new permanent force will be added to make the object bounce.

eventsFunctions.name: BounceOffVertically
eventsFunctions.description:
Bounce the object off another object it is currently colliding with, according to the angle and the speed of forces applied on the object.
The bounce will always be vertical, like if the object is *colliding a perfectly horizontal obstacle* (like the top/bottom of the screen in a pong game). For example, if the object is arriving with an angle of exactly 90 degrees, it will bounce in the opposite direction: -90 degrees.

Make sure to test for a collision between the two objects before launching this action. All the forces will be removed from the object, and a new permanent force will be added to make the object bounce.

eventsFunctions.name: BounceOffHorizontally
eventsFunctions.description:
Bounce the object off another object it is currently colliding with, according to the angle and the speed of forces applied on the object.
The bounce will always be horizontal, like if the object is *colliding a perfectly vertical obstacle* (like paddles in a pong game). For example, if the object is arriving with an angle of exactly 0 degrees, it will bounce in the opposite direction: 180 degrees.

Make sure to test for a collision between the two objects before launching this action. All the forces will be removed from the object, and a new permanent force will be added to make the object bounce.

eventsFunctions.name: BounceCount
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the number of times this object has bounced off another object.

extensionShortHeaders.name: InAppPurchase
extensionShortHeaders.shortDescription: In-app purchases for Android/iOS: list products, buy, and restore purchases.

eventsFunctions.name: RegisterItem
eventsFunctions.description:
Register a Product of your store. This is required to do for all products you want to display or order from the app. 

Make sure you register them all and finalize registration before ordering a product.

eventsFunctions.name: FinalizeRegistration
eventsFunctions.description: Finalize store registration. Do this after registering every product and before ordering or getting information about a product.

eventsFunctions.name: OrderItem
eventsFunctions.description:
Opens the purchase menu to let the user buy a product.

Ensure you use the condition to check if the store is ready and that the product ID has been registered and finalized before calling this action.

eventsFunctions.name: GetProduct
eventsFunctions.fullName: Load product data in a variable
eventsFunctions.description:
Get all the data about a product from the IAP provider and store it into a structure variable.
Check [this page](https://github.com/j3k0/cordova-plugin-purchase/blob/master/api/classes/CdvPurchase.Product.md) for the exhaustive list of what can be retrieved from the product.

eventsFunctions.name: WatchItemEvent
eventsFunctions.fullName: Update a variable when a product event is triggered
eventsFunctions.description:
When an event is triggered for a product (approved or finished), this sets a scene variable to true. 
You can then compare the value of the variable in a condition, and have actions launched to react to the changes.
Use with Trigger Once to avoid registering multiple watchers unnecessarily.

Approved is triggered after the purchase is complete.
Finished is triggered after you have marked the purchased as delivered (less useful).

eventsFunctions.name: FinalizePurchase
eventsFunctions.description: Mark a purchase as delivered, after you delivered the rewards the user has paid for and saved it somewhere. If you don't do so, the user will get the money refunded as the purchase will be considered as incomplete, with the rewards not given.

eventsFunctions.name: StoreReady
eventsFunctions.description: Triggers after finalizing the registration. Products can then be retrieved and purchased (you can get data of a product like the price, you can use the action to order a product...).

eventsFunctions.description: Generate a number between -1 and 1 from 1 dimensional simplex noise. The "Map" expression from Extended Math extension can be used to map values to any chosen bounds.

eventsFunctions.description: Delete a noise generators and loose its settings.

eventsFunctions.name: DeleteAll
eventsFunctions.description: Delete all noise generators and loose their settings.

eventsFunctions.name: SetSeed
eventsFunctions.description: The seed is a number used to generate the random noise. Setting the same seed will result in the same random noise generation. It's for example useful to generate the same world, by saving this seed value and reusing it later to generate again a world.

eventsFunctions.name: SetLoopPeriodX
eventsFunctions.description: Change the looping period on X used for noise generation. The noise will wrap-around on X.

eventsFunctions.name: SetLoopPeriodY
eventsFunctions.description: Change the looping period on Y used for noise generation. The noise will wrap-around on Y.

eventsFunctions.description: Change the base frequency used for noise generation. A lower frequency will zoom in the noise.

eventsFunctions.name: SetOctaves
eventsFunctions.description: Change the number of octaves used for noise generation. It can be seen as layers of noise with different zoom.

eventsFunctions.name: SetPersistence
eventsFunctions.description: Change the persistence used for noise generation. At its default value "0.5", it halves the noise amplitude at each octave.

eventsFunctions.name: SetLacunarity
eventsFunctions.description: Change the lacunarity used for noise generation. At its default value "2", it doubles the frequency at each octave.

headers.name: FPSObjectParenting
headers.description: Automatically handles the complex math required to attach 3D objects (viewmodels, weapons, items) to the player's 3D camera. It supports linear and angular interpolation (lerping) for realistic weapon "weight" and sway.

headers.name: FpsAnimation
headers.description: Automates procedural animations for viewmodels (like weapons). It uses mathematical oscillators to simulate walking movement and provides interpolation actions to smoothly transition between states, such as moving from a "Hip-fire" position to an "Aim-Down-Sights" (ADS) position.

headers.name: RevoluteJoint
headers.description:
Create and manage revolute joints between two objects.
Also known as a hinge joint because the objects act like they are connected with a hinge that pivots.

headers.name: WeldJoint
headers.description:
Create and manage weld joints between two objects.
Also known as a static joint because the objects stay glued in the same relative position to each other.

headers.name: DistanceJoint
headers.description:
Create and manage distance joints between two objects. 
Acts like a spring because the objects try to stay the same distance apart.

headers.name: RopeJoint
headers.description:
Create and manage rope joints between two objects. 
Objects can get closer, but they cannot exceed the starting distance from each other.

headers.name: MazeObject
headers.description: This behavior adds some helper functions to be used with Maze Generator extension, that can be used in the event sheet. Search for "Maze" in the events editor to find them after attaching this to an object. Objects with this behavior need to be used with the "CreateMazeFloorObject", "CreateMazeWallObjects" or "CreateCornerObjects" functions of the maze generator in order to benefit from the helper functions. For example, "CheckMazeObjecetLocation" condition filters objects from a row number and column number of a maze.
